title: Tapping for "Who Am I to Have So Much?"
subtitle: An EFT sequence for the fear that wealth will make you a target or change who you are
excerpt: An EFT tapping sequence for the belief that significant wealth is dangerous, unbecoming, or will turn you into someone who deserves to be judged. Taps through the ceiling that holds money at a safe, uncontroversial level and toward the permission to have significantly more.
sourceNotes: Written for homemade.education. EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s).

practice.whenToUse: Use when financial growth triggers fear of judgement, visibility, or the loss of identity — when having more feels like a risk to who you are.

content.text: An EFT tapping sequence for the ceiling that keeps money at a safe, uncontroversial level. Taps through the belief that significant wealth will make you a target, change who you are at the core, or give others grounds for judgement.
New to tapping? Read
how tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Tap the karate chop point on the side of your hand and say each statement once:
Even though part of me believes that having a lot of money makes me someone who can be judged, I accept where I am with this.
Even though I hold myself at a certain ceiling because more feels like a target, there is something underneath that knows differently.
Even though I shrink from more because of what people would say, I am open to a different relationship with this.
Tapping round
Tap each point seven times while saying the phrase:
Eyebrow: Who am I to have so much?
Side of Eye: What would people say?
Under Eye: Money changes you, and not for the better.
Under Nose: Rich women are targets.
Chin: If I have too much, I will be judged.
Collarbone: I hold myself at a certain ceiling.
Under Arm: Having a lot feels unsafe.
Top of Head: Who am I to want that much?
Reframe
Eyebrow: My wealth is mine to have.
Side of Eye: Having more doesn't change who I am at the core.
Under Eye: I can be wealthy and still be myself.
Under Nose: My values hold when the money arrives.
Chin: I can hold more and still be good.
Collarbone: Wealth is a tool, not a verdict on my character.
Under Arm: I am allowed to have a lot.
Top of Head: I can be wealthy and kind, wealthy and still me.
Write down one thing you would do with significantly more money that reflects exactly who you already are.
Where this comes from
Written for homemade.education. EFT framework developed by Gary Craig (mid-1990s).
